lessons.name: The Core Three-Shape Vowels
lessons.description: Characters built from the primary '3' shape with structural extensions, horizontal top bars, and right-hand vertical anchors.

lessons.name: Three-Shape Variations
lessons.description: Letters derived from the '3' baseline shape that omit the full right-hand vertical anchor structure.

lessons.name: The Narrow Loop and Spine
lessons.description: Forms featuring a sharp diagonal or narrow hook that drops down and anchors to the right or left.

lessons.name: The Vertical Loop Anchor
lessons.description: Characters constructed with an enclosed circular loop intersecting a central vertical hanging stroke.

lessons.name: Right-Angle Hanging Frames
lessons.description: Glyphs containing sharp, square bends or suspended right-angle hooks dropping from the top bar.

lessons.name: Left-Facing Open Hooks
lessons.description: Letters containing distinct leftward-opening loops or curved vertical stems.

lessons.name: C-Curves and Enclosures
lessons.description: Characters featuring small vertical drop-lines that break into broad rightward 'C' curves and internal knots.

lessons.name: S-Curves and Loops
lessons.description: Characters sharing a tight, fluid double-curve or vertical serpentine baseline stroke, building upon the simple S-curve.

lessons.name: Hanging Horizontal Knots
lessons.description: Glyphs built from sharp left-to-right linear extensions, tracking hooks, and central intersections.

lessons.name: Split Top-Bar Curvatures
lessons.description: Characters that break the continuous top horizontal bar via decorative loops or distinct double-crested curves.

lessons.name: Rounded Apex and Anchors
lessons.description: Letters relying on sweeping internal terminal arcs or split downward legs connected to vertical stems.

lessons.name: Asymmetrical Waves
lessons.description: Complex shapes emphasizing broad horizontal heart-like waves, outer hooks, or non-uniform paths.

lessons.name: The Compound Ligatures
lessons.description: Complex conjunct structures formed by the physical blending of distinct consonant shapes.
